exercises.title: Course 1 Concepts Quiz
exercises.description: Demonstrate mastery of Course 1 core concepts. Complete each TODO using the correct Python concept.
exercises.starterCode:
# 1. String operations
word = "Python"
# TODO: Print the length, first char, last char, and reversed word
print(len(word))
print(word[0])
print(word[-1])
print(word[::-1])

# 2. Math operators
# TODO: Print 17 // 5 and 17 % 5
print(17 // 5)
print(17 % 5)

# 3. f-string
name = "Alice"
age = 22
# TODO: Print 'Hello, Alice! You are 22 years old.'
print(f"Hello, {name}! You are {age} years old.")
exercises.expectedOutput:
6
P
n
nohtyP
3
2
Hello, Alice! You are 22 years old.
hints: len() counts characters; indexing uses []; slicing uses [::-1] for reversal.

exercises.title: Mini Calculator
exercises.description: Build a mini calculator that performs multiple operations on two given numbers and displays the results in a formatted summary.
exercises.starterCode:
# Given numbers
num1 = 45.0
num2 = 6.0

# TODO: Calculate sum, difference, product, quotient, floor div, remainder, power
sum_result = num1 + num2
difference = num1 - num2
product = num1 * num2
quotient = num1 / num2
floor_div = num1 // num2
remainder = num1 % num2
power = num1 ** 2

# TODO: Print formatted results matching exactly:
# === Calculator Results ===
# 45.0 + 6.0 = 51.0
# 45.0 - 6.0 = 39.0
# 45.0 * 6.0 = 270.0
# 45.0 / 6.0 = 7.5
# 45.0 // 6.0 = 7.0
# 45.0 % 6.0 = 3.0
# 45.0 ** 2 = 2025.0
print("=== Calculator Results ===")
print(f"{num1} + {num2} = {sum_result}")
print(f"{num1} - {num2} = {difference}")
print(f"{num1} * {num2} = {product}")
print(f"{num1} / {num2} = {quotient}")
print(f"{num1} // {num2} = {floor_div}")
print(f"{num1} % {num2} = {remainder}")
print(f"{num1} ** 2 = {power}")
hints: All operations are defined — just call print() with f-strings.
The output format must match exactly, including spaces around operators.

exercises.title: Complete Profile Generator
exercises.description: Combine all Course 1 skills to generate a complete formatted profile. Use string methods, f-strings, math, and clean code style.
exercises.starterCode:
# Student data
first_name = "  alice  "
last_name = "  JOHNSON  "
year_of_birth = 2002
current_year = 2024
grade1 = 85.0
grade2 = 92.0
grade3 = 78.0

# TODO: Clean up names using strip() and title()
clean_first = first_name.strip().title()
clean_last = last_name.strip().title()

# TODO: Calculate age and average grade
age = current_year - year_of_birth
average = (grade1 + grade2 + grade3) / 3

# TODO: Generate username: lowercase first initial + lowercase last name
username = (clean_first[0] + clean_last).lower()

# TODO: Print the profile
print("=" * 30)
print(f"Name:     {clean_first} {clean_last}")
print(f"Age:      {age}")
print(f"Username: {username}")
print(f"Average:  {average:.1f}")
print("=" * 30)
hints: strip() removes whitespace; title() capitalizes each word.
username = (first[0] + last).lower() combines the initial with the last name.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Course 1 Review
warmup.description: Test all the key concepts from Course 1 in one block of code.
warmup.starterCode:
# Course 1 key concepts in one block
name = "Alice"
age = 22
print(f"Hello, {name}! You are {age} years old.")
print(f"In 10 years you'll be {age + 10}.")
word = "Python"
print(f"{word}: length={len(word)}, first={word[0]}, reversed={word[::-1]}")
print(17 // 5, "remainder", 17 % 5)
warmup.expectedOutput:
Hello, Alice! You are 22 years old.
In 10 years you'll be 32.
Python: length=6, first=P, reversed=nohtyP
3 remainder 2
